Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-05-2026 Text Ruling

Defendant And Cross- Complaintant Albina Khazan?S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar May-01-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT AND CROSS-COMPLAINTANT ALBINA KHAZAN'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS

Appearances via Zoom:
Wesley Holl of Lopez Law Group (925.204.6378, wesley@lopezlit.com) and Chris Wolcott of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP (925.357.3456, chris.wolcott@lewisbrisbois.com) for moving party Albina Khazan;
Celine Nguyen of Wood Litigation (415.247.7900, cnguyen@woodlitigation.com) for opposing party Jian Xu.

Argued and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Albina Khazan's motion to compel further responses to certain requests for production of documents is granted in part and denied in part.

Request for Production No. 26 seeks "Any and all photographs, videos, charts, drawings, diagrams, or depictions that show any of the conditions at the PREMISES as alleged in YOUR Complaint that led to the alleged damages." The motion for further response is denied as to this RFP because it duplicates RFP No. 1. Khazan appears to concede the duplication but justifies it because the statutory time limit expired to move to compel further responses to her previous set. This is not an adequate basis to re-propound the same interrogatory, and a motion to compel compliance with a prior representation of compliance in response to an RFP pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure, sec. 2031.320, does not have a time limit.

Request for Production No. 31 seeks "Any and all DOCUMENTS referring or relating to COMMUNICATIONS YOU had with any other PERSON about any alleged conditions of the PREMISES or ALBINA KHAZAN." Here the court disagrees that the RFP is objectionable on the basis of its duplication of Request No. 6. Xu further states that she has already produced all responsive documents, but this is not included in her verified answer and her answer is ambiguous as to whether any responsive documents are being withheld pursuant to objection. Xu shall amend: if she has already produced all responsive nonprivileged documents, she must so state in a verified amended response.

Request for Production No. 32 seeks "Any and all DOCUMENTS which are photographs or videos near the PREMISES relating to the INCIDENT." Xu asserts this request duplicates two prior RFPs but does not provide them to the court or recite them in her opposition separate statement so that the court can decide whether there is impermissible duplication. Xu shall amend her response, having failed to carry her burden on her objection.

Request for Production No. 33 seeks "Any and all DOCUMENTS which are photographs or videos of ALBINA KHAZAN's property relating to the INCIDENT." Xu asserts this request duplicates two prior RFPs but does not provide them to the court or recite them in her opposition separate statement so that the court can decide whether there is impermissible duplication. Xu shall amend her response, having failed to carry her burden on her objection.

Request for Production No. 34 seeks "Any and all DOCUMENTS which are photographs or videos of the alleged 'permanent structures' which encroached upon the property line between YOU and ALBINA KHAZAN." This request duplicates No. 16. No further response is ordered.

The court has granted partial relief. It concludes that both parties acted without substantial justification in part and does not award sanctions. Xu shall amend as ordered within two weeks.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Disqualify Counsel
Off Calendar Jan-13-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Celine Nguyen for plaintiff and cross-defendant Jian Xu (via Zoom).
Douglas Akay and Wesley Holl for defendant and cross-complainant Albina Khazan, who was also present (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on Plaintiff's Motion to Disqualify Counsel.

The Court inquired regarding the status of Douglas Akay's representation of Albina Khazan. Mr. Akay stated that a Substitution of Attorney had been executed by himself and Ms. Khazan and would be submitted for filing.

After hearing from Ms. Nguyen, Mr. Akay, and Ms. Khazan, the Court found that Mr. Akay was no longer involved in the matter and concluded that the motion to disqualify was moot.

The Court addressed the request for sanctions and stated that sanctions would not be imposed against Ms. Khazan. No sanctions were ordered.

The Court ruled as follows:

PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO DISQUALIFY COUNSEL is moot.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
